items.question: How does the spatial sound generator work?
items.answer: Our spatial sound generator uses a sophisticated multi-stage process to transform text into high-quality sound spatial. The system first analyzes your description using natural language processing to understand desired 3D audio characteristics. It breaks down your input into key components, identifying elements like positioning, distance, direction, and acoustic environment. The generator then processes this information through neural networks specifically trained for sound spatial synthesis. These networks consider frequency spectrum, amplitude modulation, and 3D characteristics. The system incorporates real-world acoustic principles to ensure naturalistic immersive audio generation. Advanced filtering and post-processing techniques optimize quality, handling complex multi-layered descriptions while maintaining professional standards for all 3D audio produced. items.question: How to write effective prompts for generating spatial sound?
items.answer: Creating effective prompts requires understanding the system's capabilities and 3D audio design principles. Start with clear, specific descriptions that include multiple layers of detail about the sound spatial you want. Instead of simple terms, provide rich descriptions such as "immersive spatial sound with 3D positioning" or "subtle spatial sound with surround effects." Include information about positioning, distance, direction, and distinctive characteristics. Our generator responds well to technical terms and acoustic properties, so include details about frequency ranges or dynamic variation. When describing complex immersive audio elements, break them down into components and specify how they interact. The more context and detail you provide, the better the generator can produce the sound spatial you're looking for. Consider the intended use and include relevant information about length or technical requirements.
